title: psoralea frutescens
intro: Psoralea frutescens, also known as the Indian turnip or wolf's bane, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to stimulate the thyroid gland and increase the production of thyroid hormones. The health benefits of Psoralea frutescens include its potential to aid in weight loss, improve skin health, and boost the immune system. Psoralea frutescens can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
